text: This book is aimed at providing a detailed, analytical overview of the European Union’s stance on and views on the on-going reform of international investment law and their implications. It provides an analysis of the EU’s approach and mechanisms in relation to its own constitution as well as the underlying politics, law and justice considerations that may explain its recent reforms. This investigation analyses in particular the rule of law and political legitimacy as the overarching concepts underpinning the EU’s efforts in relation to investment law and examines whether there are any challenges in connection with promoting the rule of law as a legitimate principle that the EU encountered when attempting to put into practice these aims. By trying to reconcile the different approaches of law and economics within the context of substantive and procedural aspects of intra-EU and extra-EU investment law and dispute settlement, this book will provide an authoritative assessment of EU's attempt to act as a legitimate standard-bearer of international rules for investment arbitration, and evaluate EU's role in the ongoing global governance debates and possible future developments of the global investment regime.
